title: OAP Presentation Intent
description: Normative schema for a Presentation Intent as described in the 'From Storefront to Canvas' whitepaper. A Presentation Intent is a structured declaration from an Agent to a Renderer describing what to show the Principal, in what arrangement, under what constraints, and with what interactive capabilities. The Renderer interprets the Intent according to its platform conventions.

presentation_id.description: Unique identifier minted by the issuing Agent. URN namespace urn:oap:presentation: is reserved for Presentation Intents.

issuer_did.description: Decentralized identifier of the Agent that generated this Presentation Intent.

principal_did.description: Decentralized identifier of the Principal for whom the presentation is intended.

items_source.description: Optional URI referencing the AQL Intent whose resolution produced the items. When present, the items array contains the resolved candidates.

layout.description: Semantic layout declaration. The Renderer interprets this according to its platform conventions.

items.description: Array of items to present. Each item references a Knowledge Node, Modality Asset, or inline data.

personalization.description: Principal context that the Renderer uses to adapt the presentation.

interaction.description: Interactive capabilities the Agent requests the Renderer to expose.

policy.description: Policy constraints governing the rendered presentation.

validity.description: Time window during which this Presentation Intent is valid. Renderers SHOULD refresh after expiry.

layout_class.description: Semantic layout class. The Renderer interprets this according to its platform.

columns.description: Suggested number of columns for grid layouts. The Renderer MAY override based on screen class.

sort_by.description: JSON Pointer to the field by which items should be initially sorted.

group_by.description: JSON Pointer to the field by which items should be grouped.

max_items_visible.description: Maximum number of items visible without scrolling or pagination. The Renderer uses this as a hint.

emphasis.description: How visual weight should be distributed across items.

item_id.description: URI of the Knowledge Node or a locally minted identifier for inline data.

type.description: Type drawn from schema.org or an OAP entity vocabulary.

include.description: JSON Pointers to fields that should be shown.

exclude.description: JSON Pointers to fields that should be hidden.

role.description: Semantic role of the asset in the presentation.

modality_assets.description: Modality Assets associated with this item, as defined in RFC 0012 section 3.5.

inline_data.description: Inline structured data when the item is not a Knowledge Node reference.

highlight.description: Whether this item should receive visual emphasis (e.g., best match, recommended).

highlight_reason.description: Human readable explanation of why this item is highlighted.

locale.description: BCP 47 language tag for the Principal's preferred locale.

wcag_level.description: Minimum WCAG conformance level the Renderer MUST satisfy.

expertise.description: Principal's expertise level. Determines information density and terminology.

screen_class.description: Target device class. The Renderer uses this to select platform appropriate rendering.

modality_preferences.description: Ordered list of preferred sensory modalities.

capability.description: Interactive capability from the closed vocabulary.

target_field.description: JSON Pointer to the field this capability acts on (for sort_by_field, filter_by_field).

callback_uri.description: OAP action endpoint invoked when the Principal exercises this capability.

inline_action.description: Inline action descriptor when no external callback is needed.

label.description: Human readable label for the interactive element in the Principal's locale.

enabled.description: Whether the capability is enabled. Disabled capabilities are rendered but not actionable.

requires_confirmation.description: Whether the Principal must confirm before the callback is invoked.

tracking.description: Permitted tracking level in the rendered presentation.

auto_play_media.description: Whether media assets may auto play. Default is false per user sovereignty.

external_resources.description: Whether the Renderer may load resources from domains other than the item origins.

sponsored_injection.description: Whether the Renderer may inject sponsored items not present in the items array. Default is false.

branding.description: Organizational branding constraints from Policy Stack Layer 3.
